sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"blue: [{min(p[0] for p in b)},{min(p[1] for p in b)}]-[{max(p[0] for p in b)},{max(p[1] for p in b)}]")
</parameter>
</function>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blue block moved UP from [44,12]-[47,31] to [40,12]-[43,31]. So it moved 4 rows up. The blue block can move in all 4 directions. Now I need to figure out the goal.
Let me think about this more carefully:
- Blue block: 4x20 rectangle, can move in all directions
- Yellow blocks: two 8x12 rectangles at the bottom
- Charcoal block: 3x4 at top [1,36]-[3,39]
- Magenta block: 4x4 at top [4,36]-[7,39]
- Green bar (N): timer at top
- Gray bar (w): bottom bar
Maybe the goal is to move the blue block to cover/collect the yellow blocks? Or maybe to position it under the charcoal/magenta blocks? Let me try moving the blue block up and to the right to position it under the charcoal/magenta blocks at [1,36]-[7,39].
The charcoal/magenta blocks are at columns 36-39. The blue block is currently at columns 12-31. I need to move it RIGHT to align with columns 36-39.
Let me try moving RIGHT several times to get the blue block to columns around 36.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move blue block right to align with charcoal/magenta at col 36-39
# Currently at col 12-31, need to move right by about 20 cols (5 steps of 4)
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
